content.text: I found the failing assertion in `src/cc65/coptstop.c` and related load-info handling in `codeoptutil.c`; next I’ll inspect the optimizer code around `Opt_a_tossub()` and the stack-op dispatcher.

I found the failing CHECK in `Opt_a_tossub()` and the relevant precondition logic that should have prevented incomplete/non-direct RHS operands from reaching that optimizer. I’ll inspect the rest of the precondition/removal handling and then reproduce locally with a temporary test.

41	/* cc65 */
42	#include "codeent.h"
43	#include "codeinfo.h"
44	#include "codeoptutil.h"
45	#include "coptstop.h"
46	#include "error.h"
47	
48	
49	
50	/*****************************************************************************/
51	/*                                   Data                                    */
52	/*****************************************************************************/
53	
54	
55	
56	/* Flags for the functions */
57	typedef enum {
58	    OP_NONE              = 0x00,         /* Nothing special */
59	    OP_A_KNOWN           = 0x01,         /* Value of A must be known */
60	    OP_X_ZERO            = 0x02,         /* X must be zero */
61	    OP_LHS_LOAD          = 0x04,         /* Must have load insns for LHS */
62	    OP_LHS_SAME_BY_OP    = 0x08,         /* Load result of LHS must be the same if relocated to op */
63	    OP_LHS_LOAD_DIRECT   = 0x0C,         /* Must have direct load insn for LHS */
64	    OP_RHS_LOAD          = 0x10,         /* Must have load insns for RHS */
65	    OP_RHS_SAME_BY_OP    = 0x20,         /* Load result of RHS must be the same if relocated to op */
66	    OP_RHS_LOAD_DIRECT   = 0x30,         /* Must have direct load insn for RHS */
67	    OP_AX_INTERCHANGE    = 0x40,         /* Preconditions of A/X may be interchanged */
68	    OP_LR_INTERCHANGE    = 0x80,         /* Preconditions of LHS/RHS may be interchanged */
69	    OP_LHS_SAME_BY_PUSH  = 0x0100,       /* LHS must load the same content if relocated to push */
70	    OP_RHS_SAME_BY_PUSH  = 0x0200,       /* RHS must load the same content if relocated to push */
71	    OP_LHS_SAME_BY_RHS   = 0x0400,       /* LHS must load the same content if relocated to RHS */
72	    OP_RHS_SAME_BY_LHS   = 0x0800,       /* RHS must load the same content if relocated to LHS */
73	    OP_LHS_REMOVE        = 0x1000,       /* LHS must be removable or RHS may use ZP store/load */
74	    OP_LHS_REMOVE_DIRECT = 0x3000,       /* LHS must be directly removable */
75	    OP_RHS_REMOVE        = 0x4000,       /* RHS must be removable or LHS may use ZP store/load */
76	    OP_RHS_REMOVE_DIRECT = 0xC000,       /* RHS must be directly removable */
77	} OP_FLAGS;
78	
79	/* Structure that describes an optimizer subfunction for a specific op */
80	typedef unsigned (*OptFunc) (StackOpData* D);
81	typedef struct OptFuncDesc OptFuncDesc;
82	struct OptFuncDesc {
83	    const char*         Name;           /* Name of the replaced runtime function */
84	    OptFunc             Func;           /* Function pointer */
85	    unsigned            UnusedRegs;     /* Regs that must not be used later */
86	    OP_FLAGS            Flags;          /* Flags */
87	};
88	
89

41	/* cc65 */
42	#include "codeent.h"
43	#include "codeseg.h"
44	
45	
46	
47	/*****************************************************************************/
48	/*                            Load tracking data                             */
49	/*****************************************************************************/
50	
51	
52	
53	/* LoadRegInfo flags set by DirectOp */
54	typedef enum {
55	    LI_NONE         = 0x00,
56	    LI_DIRECT       = 0x01,         /* Direct op may be used */
57	    LI_RELOAD_Y     = 0x02,         /* Reload index register Y */
58	    LI_REMOVE       = 0x04,         /* Load may be removed */
59	    LI_DONT_REMOVE  = 0x08,         /* Load may not be removed */
60	    LI_CHECK_ARG    = 0x10,         /* Load src might be modified later */
61	    LI_CHECK_Y      = 0x20,         /* Indexed load src might be modified later */
62	    LI_SRC_USE      = 0x40,         /* src of Opc argument is possibly used */
63	    LI_SRC_CHG      = 0x80,         /* src of Opc argument is possibly modified */
64	    LI_Y_SRC_USE    = 0x0100,       /* src of Opc addressing Y is possibly used */
65	    LI_Y_SRC_CHG    = 0x0200,       /* src of Opc addressing Y is possibly modified */
66	    LI_Y_USE        = 0x0400,       /* Opc addressing Y is possibly used */
67	    LI_Y_CHG        = 0x0800,       /* Opc addressing Y is possibly modified */
68	    LI_USED_BY_A    = 0x1000,       /* Content used by RegA */
69	    LI_USED_BY_X    = 0x2000,       /* Content used by RegX */
70	    LI_USED_BY_Y    = 0x4000,       /* Content used by RegY */
71	    LI_SP           = 0x8000,       /* Content on stack */
72	    LI_LOAD_INSN    = 0x010000,     /* Is a load insn */
73	} LI_FLAGS;
74	
75	/* Structure that tells us how to load the lhs values */
76	typedef struct LoadRegInfo LoadRegInfo;
77	struct LoadRegInfo {
78	    LI_FLAGS        Flags;          /* Tells us how to load */
79	    int             LoadIndex;      /* Index of load insn, -1 if invalid */
80	    CodeEntry*      LoadEntry;      /* The actual entry, 0 if invalid */
81	    int             LoadYIndex;     /* Index of Y-load insn, -1 if invalid  */
82	    CodeEntry*      LoadYEntry;     /* The actual Y-load entry, 0 if invalid */
83	    int             ChgIndex;       /* Index of last change */
84	    CodeEntry*      ChgEntry;       /* The actual change entry */
85	    int             Offs;           /* Stack offset if data is on stack */
86	};
87	
88	/* Now combined for both registers */
89	typedef struct LoadInfo LoadInfo;
90	struct LoadInfo {
91	    LoadRegInfo     A;              /* Info for A register */
92	    LoadRegInfo     X;              /* Info for X register */
93	    LoadRegInfo     Y;              /* Info for Y register */
94	};

content.text: I found the relevant code path: `Opt_a_tossub()` requires both RHS byte loads to be direct/removable, while the RegA-only precondition checker currently only validates the low-byte RHS. I’ll add a temporary reproducer next so we can confirm the failure and validate the fix.

;
; File generated by cc65 v 2.19 - Git 4232e7acc
;
	.fopt		compiler,"cc65 v 2.19 - Git 4232e7acc"
	.setcpu		"6502"
	.smart		on
	.autoimport	on
	.case		on
	.debuginfo	off
	.importzp	c_sp, sreg, regsave, regbank
	.importzp	tmp1, tmp2, tmp3, tmp4, ptr1, ptr2, ptr3, ptr4
	.macpack	longbranch
	.forceimport	__STARTUP__
	.export		_a
	.export		_b
	.export		_c
	.export		_d
	.export		_main

.segment	"DATA"

_b:
	.byte	$00
_c:
	.byte	$0A
_d:
	.byte	$01

.segment	"BSS"

_a:
	.res	1,$00

; ---------------------------------------------------------------
; int __near__ main (void)
; ---------------------------------------------------------------

.segment	"CODE"

.proc	_main: near

.segment	"CODE"

;
; if (b != 0) {
;
	lda     _b
	beq     L0003
;
; b = 0;
;
	lda     #$00
	sta     _b
;
; a = c - (d >= 0);
;
L0003:	sec
	sbc     #$01
	sta     _a
;
; return !(a == 9);
;
	cmp     #$09
	jmp     boolne

.endproc

file.content:
# ---- Display info during parsing phase ----
SILENT:=$(findstring s,$(word 1, $(MAKEFLAGS)))
ifneq ($(SILENT),s)
    $(info Using Makefile: $(realpath $(firstword $(MAKEFILE_LIST))) $(MAKECMDGOALS))
endif

# Makefile for the regression tests that return an error code on failure

ifneq ($(shell echo),)
  CMD_EXE = 1
endif

ifdef CMD_EXE
  S = $(subst /,\,/)
  NULLDEV = nul:
  MKDIR = mkdir $(subst /,\,$1)
  RMDIR = -rmdir /s /q $(subst /,\,$1)
  CATRES =
else
  S = /
  NULLDEV = /dev/null
  MKDIR = mkdir -p $1
  RMDIR = $(RM) -r $1
  CATRES = > $(WORKDIR)/$$@.out 2> $(WORKDIR)/$$@.out || (cat $(WORKDIR)/$$@.out && false)
endif

ifeq ($(SILENT),s)
    QUIET = 1
endif

ifdef QUIET
  .SILENT:
  NULLOUT = >$(NULLDEV)
  NULLERR = 2>$(NULLDEV)
ifndef CMD_EXE
  CATERR = 2> $(WORKDIR)/$$@.errlog || (cat $(WORKDIR)/$$@.errlog && false)
endif
endif

SIM65FLAGS = -x 4000000000 -c

CC65 := $(if $(wildcard ../../bin/cc65*),..$S..$Sbin$Scc65,cc65)
CA65 := $(if $(wildcard ../../bin/ca65*),..$S..$Sbin$Sca65,ca65)
LD65 := $(if $(wildcard ../../bin/ld65*),..$S..$Sbin$Sld65,ld65)
SIM65 := $(if $(wildcard ../../bin/sim65*),..$S..$Sbin$Ssim65,sim65)

WORKDIR = ../../testwrk/val

OPTIONS = g O Os Osi Osir Osr Oi Oir Or

.PHONY: all clean

SOURCES := $(wildcard *.c)
TESTS  = $(foreach option,$(OPTIONS),$(SOURCES:%.c=$(WORKDIR)/%.$(option).6502.prg))
TESTS += $(foreach option,$(OPTIONS),$(SOURCES:%.c=$(WORKDIR)/%.$(option).65c02.prg))

all: $(TESTS)

$(WORKDIR):
	$(call MKDIR,$(WORKDIR))

define PRG_template

$(WORKDIR)/%.$1.$2.prg: %.c | $(WORKDIR)
	$(if $(QUIET),echo val/$$*.$1.$2.prg)
	$(CC65) -t sim$2 $$(CC65FLAGS) --add-source -$1 -o $$(@:.prg=.s) $$< $(NULLOUT) $(CATERR)
	$(CA65) -t sim$2 -o $$(@:.prg=.o) $$(@:.prg=.s) $(NULLOUT) $(CATERR)
	$(LD65) -t sim$2 -o $$@ $$(@:.prg=.o) sim$2.lib $(NULLOUT) $(CATERR)
	$(SIM65) $(SIM65FLAGS) $$@ $(CATRES)

endef # PRG_template

$(foreach option,$(OPTIONS),$(eval $(call PRG_template,$(option),6502)))
$(foreach option,$(OPTIONS),$(eval $(call PRG_template,$(option),65c02)))

clean:
	@$(call RMDIR,$(WORKDIR))

57:all: $(TESTS)

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `src/cc65/coptstop.c`.

Summary:
- Found the assertion path in `RegAPreCondOk()` / `Opt_a_tossub()`.
- Reproduced the failure with the provided minimal case under `-O`.
- Fixed the precondition check for RegA-only stack optimizations so optimizations requiring direct RHS removal also reject cases where the RHS high byte is not directly usable/removable.
- Rebuilt `cc65`.
- Verified the temporary reproducer now compiles, links, and runs successfully under optimization.
- Removed the temporary bug2946 test files/artifacts from `mnt_tmp`.

Validation run:
- `make -C /testbed/src -j2`
- Optimized compile/link/run of the temporary bug2946 reproducer with `sim6502`
- Nearby validation target `bug1989.O.6502.prg` checked

Only source change left:
- `src/cc65/coptstop.c`

No commits were made.
